steps.title: Identify what "emphasize when" requires
steps.content: The question asks which choice best emphasizes when Goldstein won the award. The notes supply one specific timing detail: 2012. A choice that buries the year in a subordinate clause or omits it entirely cannot be the answer. The correct choice must foreground 2012 as the focal point of the sentence.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice against the emphasis test
steps.content:
Test each option for whether it places the year 2012 in a prominent, focal position:
  • Choice A — For transformative work that laid the foundations for digital cryptography, Rina Goldstein won the A.M. Turing Award.: emphasizes the reason for the award, not the timing — no year appears. ✗
  • Choice B — It was in 2012 that Rina Goldstein won the A.M. Turing Award.: the cleft construction "It was in 2012 that..." places the year at the front as the sole focus of the sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — Rina Goldstein is one winner of the A.M. Turing Award.: merely identifies her as a winner with no year mentioned. ✗
  • Choice D — The prestigious A.M. Turing Award is given for "major contributions of lasting importance to computing.": describes the award itself; says nothing about when Goldstein won. ✗
That is the correct answer.
